title: Contacts for WHMIS - Canada.ca
content:
General Workplace Hazardous Materials Information System (WHMIS) questions, including about the implementation of the Globally Harmonized System of Classification and Labelling of Chemicals (GHS) for workplace chemicals in Canada, can be directed to Health Canada .
WHMIS News is an email list used by Health Canada to distribute the latest news and information relating to WHMIS as it becomes available.
Subscribe to WHMIS Mailing List

## Occupational Health and Safety Regulators
For information on employer or worker obligations under WHMIS visit WHMIS.org or the federal, provincial, or territorial occupational health and safety regulatory agency in their jurisdiction.
- Alberta Occupational health and safety (OHS)
- British Columbia WorksafeBC
- Manitoba Labour and Immigration - Workplace Safety and Health
- New Brunswick WorksafeNB
- Newfoundland and Labrador Occupational Health & Safety
- Northwest Territories and Nunavut Workers' Safety & Compensation Commission
- Nova Scotia Labour, Skills and Immigration – Health and Safety
- Ontario Workplace health and safety
- Prince Edward Island Safety Matters @ Work - Workers Compensation Board of PEI
- Québec Commission des normes, de l’équité, de la santé et de la sécurité du travail (CNESST)
- Saskatchewan Safety in the Workplace
- Yukon Territories Yukon Workers' Compensation Health and Safety Board
- Workplace standards – Employment and Social Development Canada (ESDC)
- Canada - Newfoundland and Labrador Offshore Petroleum Board
- Canada - Nova Scotia Offshore Petroleum Board
